---
title: "OLS and Elastic Net"
author: "Erica"
date: "2026-03-11"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE
)
```   


```{r}
#load library
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(lubridate)
library(glmnet)
library(ggplot2)
library(lmtest)
```


```{r}
#read in data
file_path = "../data/df_for_shap_analysis.csv"
df = read.csv(file_path) 
```

## ols function to be copied to replace the code block in eval
```{r}
predict_alpha_ols <- function(train_df){
  df <- train_df %>%
    mutate(
      caldt = as.Date(caldt),
      year = year(caldt)
    ) %>%
    arrange(year, crsp_fundno) %>%
    drop_na()

  evaluation_year <- max(df$year)

  train_data <- df %>%
    filter(year < evaluation_year)

  test_data <- df %>%
    filter(year == evaluation_year)

  feature_names <- train_data %>%
    select(-alpha_ann, -crsp_fundno, -caldt, -year) %>%
    colnames()

  formula <- as.formula(
    paste("alpha_ann ~", paste(feature_names, collapse = " + "))
  )

  model <- lm(formula, data = train_data) # no hyperparameter tuning is needed for OLS

  preds <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)

  result <- tibble(
    evaluation_year = evaluation_year,
    crsp_fundno = test_data$crsp_fundno,
    predicted_alpha = preds
  )

  return(result)
}
```

## feature importance of OLS
```{r}
# preprocess
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    caldt = as.Date(caldt),
    year = lubridate::year(caldt)
  ) %>%
  arrange(year, crsp_fundno) %>%
  drop_na()

# train-test split
train_df <- df %>% filter(year <= 2024, year >= 2004)

predict_alpha_ols_with_model <- function(train_df){
  
  df <- train_df %>%
    mutate(
      caldt = as.Date(caldt),
      year = lubridate::year(caldt)
    ) %>%
    arrange(year, crsp_fundno) %>%
    drop_na()

  evaluation_year <- max(df$year)

  train_data <- df %>% filter(year < evaluation_year)

  feature_names <- train_data %>%
    select(-alpha_ann, -crsp_fundno, -caldt, -year) %>%
    colnames()

  formula <- as.formula(
    paste("alpha_ann ~", paste(feature_names, collapse = " + "))
  )

  model <- lm(formula, data = train_data)

  return(list(
    model = model,
    train_data = train_data,
    feature_names = feature_names
  ))
}

ols_output <- predict_alpha_ols_with_model(train_df)

model <- ols_output$model
train_data <- ols_output$train_data
feature_names <- ols_output$feature_names

X_train <- train_data %>% 
  select(all_of(feature_names)) %>% 
  as.matrix()

# ---------------------------
# SHAP
# ---------------------------

coefs <- coef(model)[-1]

# ensure alignment
coefs <- coefs[colnames(X_train)]

mu <- colMeans(X_train)

# SHAP = beta * (x - mean)
shap_values <- sweep(X_train, 2, mu, "-") %>%
  sweep(2, coefs, "*") %>%
  as.data.frame()

# feature importance
abs_shap <- abs(shap_values)
abs_shap_mean <- colMeans(abs_shap) %>%
  sort(decreasing = TRUE)

importance_df <- tibble(
  feature = names(abs_shap_mean),
  importance = abs_shap_mean
)

# plot
ols_featimp_ranking <- 
  ggplot(importance_df, 
         aes(y = reorder(feature, importance),
             x = importance,
             fill = importance)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "firebrick", high = "dodgerblue4") +
  labs(
    title = "OLS SHAP Ranking",
    y = "",
    x = "Mean (|SHAP|)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ols_featimp_ranking

ggsave("../figures/ols_shap_plot.png", width = 16, height = 12)
```
